title: Minestrone

overview.description: A classic Italian vegetable soup made with seasonal vegetables, beans and pasta in a light tomato broth. Healthy, adaptable and an excellent way to use whatever vegetables are available.

ingredients: 150g green beans, trimmed and cut into bite-sized pieces
100g small pasta (ditalini, small shells or macaroni)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
Extra virgin olive oil, to serve
method: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over a medium heat. Cook the onion, carrot and celery for about 10 minutes until softened.
Add the garlic, courgette and green beans. Cook for a further 5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato purée and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped tomatoes, cannellini beans and stock. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 20 minutes.
Add the pasta and cook until almost tender.
Stir in the peas and spinach during the final 3–4 minutes of cooking.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve topped with Parmesan, fresh parsley and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

learning.did-you-know: There is no single authentic minestrone recipe. Across Italy, families traditionally use whatever vegetables are in season, making every version slightly different.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build an Italian vegetable soup, cook pasta directly in broth, and adapt recipes to make the most of seasonal produce.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A versatile Pantry Monday recipe that's inexpensive, nutritious and ideal for using up vegetables before they spoil.
chapman-tips: Use whatever seasonal vegetables you have available.
Add delicate vegetables like peas and spinach right at the end to keep their colour.
Cook the pasta just until al dente to prevent it becoming too soft.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze before adding the pasta for the best texture. Cook fresh pasta when reheating.
notes.leftovers: The soup thickens overnight as the pasta absorbs liquid. Add a splash of stock or water when reheating.
